title: Bridge Essay: Decoding Natural Sounds in Early "Visual" Cortex of Congenitally Blind Individuals.
concept: Mental imagery as real perception — imagination, pareidolia, aphantasia.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal Reconstruction** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Imaginal — mental imagery as real perception, imagination activating sensory cortices, aphantasia.
**Phase context:** Mental imagery as real perception — imagination, pareidolia, aphantasia.

**Decoding Natural Sounds in Early "Visual" Cortex of Congenitally Blind Individuals.** — Curr Biol (2020). DOI: 10.1016/j.cub.2020.05.071. PMID: 32559449. Complex natural sounds, such as bird singing, people talking, or traffic noise, induce decodable fMRI activation patterns in early visual cortex of sighted blindfolded participants [1]. That is, early visual cortex receives non-visual and potentially predictive information from audition.
